text.title: Two-pass: free reasoning then strict extraction
text.summary: Run hard reasoning in free text on the strong model, then extract into the strict schema with a cheap model.

section.title: The rule
section.p: For tasks where reasoning quality matters more than output format, never lock the reasoning step into strict structured output. Split the work into two passes: pass 1 produces free-form reasoning on the strong model, pass 2 extracts the structured answer with a cheap model.

rule.statement: When a task requires deep reasoning AND a strict schema, run the strong model in free-text or extended-thinking mode for the reasoning step, then run a cheap extractor model with strict structured output to fill the schema from the strong model's transcript.
rule.rationale: The grammar mask of strict JSON costs probability mass on schema-conforming tokens that are not the optimal reasoning tokens. The SLOT paper and the BuildMVPFast 2026 guide both report 10–15 percent accuracy loss on math and complex analysis when reasoning is forced through strict SO versus free text. Two-pass restores accuracy because the reasoning step has no schema constraint, and the extraction step is easy enough that a small model meets the spec at fraction of the cost.

example.code:
# Pass 1: Opus thinks freely. raw = anthropic.messages.create( model="claude-opus-4-7", thinking={"type": "enabled", "budget_tokens": 16000}, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": question}], ).content[-1].text
# Pass 2: Haiku extracts into strict schema. verdict = haiku.responses.parse( model="claude-haiku-4-7", input=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Extract verdict from analysis:\n{raw}"}], text_format=Verdict, # strict Pydantic model )
example.note: Reasoning is unconstrained and benefits from extended thinking. Extraction is mechanical, so a Haiku-class model meets the schema reliably and cheaply.

example.code: # Single pass: Opus forced into strict JSON during reasoning. verdict = anthropic.messages.create( model="claude-opus-4-7", tools=[{"name": "submit_verdict", "input_schema": Verdict.model_json_schema()}], tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "submit_verdict"}, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": question}], )
example.note: Strict mode masks tokens during the reasoning trace itself. Accuracy drops 10–15 percent on hard tasks because the model cannot fully reason inside the grammar.

antipattern.description: Adding a long reasoning field to a strict schema as a substitute for two-pass on math or research tasks.
antipattern.reason: The embedded scratchpad pattern works for medium-difficulty reasoning, but on truly hard tasks the grammar mask still degrades the reasoning trace. When task difficulty saturates the scratchpad, switch to two-pass.

section.reference: Reusable two-pass function with Opus reasoning and Haiku extraction.
SLOT — Structuring the Output of Large Language Models (arXiv 2505.04016).
BuildMVPFast — Structured output 2026 guide (10–15% accuracy gap).

see-also.link: When two-pass actually saves money vs single-pass strict SO.
